summary: Phase 3.9 RETEST after middleware fix. SKIP_PATHS regex `^/api/users/(me|[^/]+)/form-preferences$` added to permissions_middleware.py — workers can now read/write their own form-preferences while still being denied on other users' prefs. Backend pytest now 14/14 PASS (xfail markers converted to hard asserts). Phase 3.8 regression 13/13 PASS. Admin /api/users router unaffected (GET /api/users, GET /api/users/{id} = 200). Worker frontend e2e smoke verified end-to-end: login → /scan/EFLdyI3Thc → gear icon → dialog opens (23 template rows + 24 checkboxes) → untick + Save → 'Preferences saved' toast → 'Filtered by your preferences · edit' label → list narrows to 3 plant forms. Worker device_only=true PUT verified server-side no-op (worker baseline 6 ids unchanged after shrink-PUT). Cleanup: admin + worker prefs restored to all-enabled (empty whitelist sentinel) — admin plant scan now returns applied_preferences=false with full curated list. Phase 3.9 GREEN.

worker_frontend_smoke_pass: [data-testid=form-prefs-dialog] opens with 23 form-prefs-row-* and 24 checkboxes (incl. device-only)
Untick first row + click [data-testid=form-prefs-save] → 'Preferences saved' toast
[data-testid=scan-forms-prefs-applied] label 'Filtered by your preferences · edit' rendered
Plant scan list narrowed to 3 forms (Plant Pre-Start RECOMMENDED, Incident Report, Near Miss Report)
test_results_summary.worker_device_only_verified: PUT /api/users/me/form-preferences {enabled_template_ids:[incident],device_only:true} → 200; GET after returns same 6 ids as before (set equality True). Server doc untouched.

critical_code_review_comments: permissions_middleware.py SKIP_PATHS regex is precise — it anchors to the `/form-preferences` suffix so generic /api/users/{id} reads/writes still flow through the users.view/edit gate. Verified live.

Suggestion (non-blocking): the SKIP_PATHS check runs before PATH_RESOURCE; current ordering is correct. Add a unit test asserting GET /api/users/{id} still hits the middleware (returns 403 for a worker on another user) so a future broadening of the regex doesn't accidentally open the whole users router.

Reset semantics: PUT with enabled_template_ids:[] clears the whitelist (sentinel meaning 'all enabled'). After cleanup, admin plant scan returns applied_preferences=false with full curated 3-form list — confirms the empty-list sentinel works as documented.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase_39_form_prefs.py (xfail markers in test_worker_can_read_own_via_user_id and test_worker_can_read_own_via_me replaced with hard `assert r.status_code == 200` + payload checks)

success_rate.frontend: 100% worker e2e smoke (gear → dialog → save → toast → applied label → filtered list)

rca_of_the_issue: Previous bug (iteration_16) RCA: PermissionsMiddleware applied a coarse `users.view`/`users.edit` check to anything matching `^/api/users(/|$)`, and worker role default is users.view=False. Fix: added `re.compile(r'^/api/users/(me|[^/]+)/form-preferences$')` to SKIP_PATHS (lines 56-62 of permissions_middleware.py). This skip is BEFORE the PATH_RESOURCE lookup so the suffix routes pass through to the handler, which enforces its own RBAC (self always allowed; admin/manager/hseq_lead for other-user reads; admin only for other-user writes). Verified by curl: worker GET/PUT /me/form-preferences = 200; worker GET other-user prefs still = 403; admin GET /api/users (and /api/users/{id}) still = 200. The regex anchor `$` is important — it prevents any other /api/users subpath from being whitelisted by accident.
